page: Amazon Web Services p4d.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8275CL processors running at 3.0 GHz. The hardware architecture includes 1152.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. For acceleration, the instance is equipped with eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs delivering a total of 320 GB of VRAM. Storage is provided via 8000 GB of local NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves a top-tier multi-core CPU score in stress-ng of 94545.39 and a PassMark CPU score of 61678.8. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block read-only cached bandwidth reaching 5252.26 GB/sec and large-block uncached write bandwidth at 98.72 GB/sec. Database operations are also strong, with Redis SET operations reaching 78.21 million operations per second and static web serving throughput measured at 42.9 GB/sec.

While multi-core, database, and memory bandwidth metrics are in the top tier, single-core performance is moderate, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1291.0 and average single-threaded compression speeds. Additionally, LLM inference text generation speeds are relatively weak, yielding 55.02 tokens/sec for a small 135M model and 8.46 tokens/sec for a medium 7B model, though prompt processing speeds remain average. This hardware profile is suited for multi-threaded compute tasks, large-scale database operations, ray tracing, and GPU-accelerated machine learning workloads that leverage parallel processing.
description: Amazon Web Services p4d.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring 96 vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8275CL processors at 3.0 GHz and 1152.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It integrates eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 320 GB of total VRAM, alongside 8000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and rapid database operations, including 78.21 million Redis SET operations per second. However, single-core processing and LLM text generation speeds are comparatively moderate to weak. This instance is configured for parallelized GPU workloads, high-throughput database systems, and multi-threaded computational tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services p4d.24xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1152 GB RAM, eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs, and 8000 GB NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services p4d.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 1152 GB RAM, eight NVIDIA A100 GPUs, and 8000 GB NVMe SSD for intensive GPU and database tasks.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance combining eight accelerators, massive system memory, and local NVMe storage for parallelized, data-intensive computational workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 320 GB total VRAM
96 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8275CL processors at 3.0 GHz
1152.0 GB DDR4 system memory and 8000 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU and high-throughput memory bandwidth performance
Suited for parallel GPU computing, large databases, and multi-threaded workloads
